category_title: Students with mild intellectual disability and leadership potential
minimal_context: Ben is using Google Drive to make a mind map defining emotion for his Liberal Studies class.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Please open Google Drive and make a mind map to work out what is the definition of emotion today.

STUDENT: I put 'HAPPY' first on the mind map.

TUTOR: Okay, keep adding to it.

STUDENT: Typing... I need to work hard.

TUTOR: Any difficulties that you might encounter?

STUDENT: I need to rest. I am feeling nervous.

options.A: Review consequence maps with Ben before the Liberal Studies session. Let him choose the review order, then read the negative consequences followed by the positive ones.
options.B: Provide noncontingent attention to Ben throughout the day, and ignore any problem behaviors that function to gain attention during the mind map activity.
options.C: Have teachers across several subjects collaborate to teach similar skills to Ben together throughout the school year during his various class sessions.
options.D: Complete the first year of instruction with Ben, covering a 25-lesson extract that addresses feelings and relationships related to the Liberal Studies topic.
